source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Elizabetta wants to write the integers 1 to 9 in the regions of the shape shown, with one integer in each region. She wants the product of the integers in any two regions that have a common edge to be not more than 15 . In how many ways can she do this? <image1>

asset_registry_record.image_manifest shows an existing image asset, and metadata includes image quality/readability information
multi_step: the task requires assigning integers 1 through 9 to regions under adjacency-based product constraints, which implies combining multiple local conditions across the whole shape
the question asks 'In how many ways', indicating a counting/combinatorial search rather than a single direct lookup
exact complexity is somewhat uncertain because the shape topology is not described in text fields and is only available via the image
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text='16', giving a clear exact target
the constraint is explicit ('product ... in any two regions that have a common edge' <= 15), so a candidate arrangement count is in principle checkable against the image-defined adjacency graph
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true and both question and answer are present
